title: Today's AI Brief: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
summary: Uber limits employee AI spending, Google fights AI deepfake scams
imageAlt: Image from source article for Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
imageCaption: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
imageSourceTitle: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. I am looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First up, Uber has put a cap on employee AI spending after blowing through their budget in just four months. I guess you could say they got a little too excited about the AI party. This move tells me that AI tools are getting more accessible, but also more expensive. Companies are figuring out how to manage these new costs, and it's a challenge many are facing.

According to TechCrunch, Uber employees went through their AI budget in record time. This rapid depletion of funds suggests that AI tools are being heavily adopted across the company. It's a bit like when you discover a new favorite coffee shop – you go back for more, and more, until you realize you've spent way too much on lattes. The big question now is how other companies will manage their AI spending.

Google is rolling out a new feature to protect against AI deepfake impersonation scams. This fake call detection will be available on Android 12+ devices this month. My read is that Google is taking a proactive approach to safety. Deepfakes are getting more convincing, and it's good to see companies working on solutions. It's a cat-and-mouse game, but with Google on the case, I feel a bit more secure.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
